title: conyza catharinensis
intro: Conyza catharinensis, also known as the Brazilian fleabane or Brazilian horseweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Conyza catharinensis include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Conyza catharinensis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
